pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project configuration must reflect the target JDK version for compilation and runtime compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 support and latest features.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Spring Boot versions may not fully support or be optimized for Java 21, and upgrading is part of the migration recipe.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Framework version to 6.1.22-atlassian-1 to match the new Spring Boot version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.3.x requires compatible Spring Framework versions; mismatched versions cause dependency resolution or runtime issues.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Micrometer version to 1.13.15 to align with the new Spring Boot version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.3.x manages Micrometer dependencies; upgrading ensures compatibility with the new Spring Boot release.

pom.xml.general_idea: Remove the exclusion of junit-vintage-engine from spring-boot-starter-test.
pom.xml.why_exists: JUnit Vintage is no longer needed or desired in the test scope, possibly because the project has fully migrated to JUnit 5.

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.general_idea: Replace deprecated Spring Security annotation with its modern replacement.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.why_exists: EnableGlobalMethodSecurity is deprecated in Spring Security 6 and removed in later versions; using it causes compilation errors or runtime warnings depending on the exact Spring Boot version, but primarily it is a breaking API change in the migration to Spring Boot 3.3 (which uses Spring Security 6.2+).

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.general_idea: Migrate Spring Security HttpSecurity configuration from the deprecated fluent API (using .and()) to the new lambda-based DSL introduced in Spring Security 5.7+.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.why_exists: The .and() method and the old fluent API for configuring HttpSecurity are deprecated and removed in newer Spring Security versions compatible with Spring Boot 3.3, causing compilation errors.

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.general_idea: Update the HttpSecurity.apply() call to use the new HttpSecurity.with() method with a Customizer argument.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.why_exists: The apply() method is deprecated/removed in favor of with() in modern Spring Security, leading to compilation failures.

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.general_idea: Migrate exceptionHandling and sessionManagement configuration blocks to the new lambda-based DSL.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.why_exists: The old fluent API for these sub-configurations is deprecated/removed, causing compilation errors.

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.general_idea: Add static import for Customizer.withDefaults() to support the new Spring Security DSL syntax.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/co.why_exists: The withDefaults() method is required by the new DSL syntax for cors() and with() calls; without the import, the code will not compile.

src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.general_idea: Replace iterator().next() with getFirst() to retrieve the first element of a list using the Java 21 API.
Replace iterator().next() with getFirst() to use the new List API introduced in Java 21.
src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.why_exists: While iterator().next() still compiles, getFirst() is the idiomatic Java 21 replacement for retrieving the first element of a List, aligning with the migration goal to use modern JDK features.

src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.general_idea: Replace iterator-based retrieval of the first element with the List.getFirst() method introduced in Java 21.
src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.why_exists: The code compiles and runs on Java 17 using the iterator pattern; this change is a modernization to use the newer API, not a fix for a compilation error.
